Module 5:
Tennis Ninja PLC 2
!
Executive Summary
Here is a summary of Will’s Tennis Ninja Launch, PLC #2.
• 	Launch Details:
• 	17,000 person list
• 	$168,328 in sales
• 	Did this less than a year after becoming a PLF Owner
• 	Will’s launches have gradually increased from $35k to $65k to $105k
to $168k (this launch) to $400k+
• 	This video is not quite as strong as the first, specifically the open and
close
• 	Where this video is very strong is the teaching portion
!
Video Notes
• 	This is the second video in the series
• 	Begins by thanking viewers for the comments
!
1	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 1 of 3 --

• 	Starts by talking about the video camera and equipment. On general I
didn’t like this, as it wasn’t focused enough on the viewer
• 	Will creates a lot of likeability for himself by making a joke and showing
off his personality
• 	Instead, tt would have been better to make the promise of what the
viewer will be getting out of this video right upfront
• 	1:10 - at this point still not loving the video’s promise to the viewer
• 	Follows up with partner talking about his camera - there’s not a lot in this
for a tennis player and this area drags on
• 	Mentions that you should go back to previous video if you haven’t seen it
- that works well
• 	Begins teaching and foreshadowing - what you’ll learn and why it’s
important
• 	Note: Make sure you’re looking at the camera mostly when you’re
teaching on a whiteboard or display
• 	Pays off on the promise from PLC #1, but waited until almost 20 minutes
into PLC #2 to deliver on the promise
• 	Did a good job or irritating and agitating about it until this point
!
!
!
2	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 2 of 3 --

Key Lessons Learned
Make sure you start every video with “what’s in it for them” - what is the
big promise? Address that first!
Creating likeability is great - be yourself
Look at the camera as much as possible when teaching
!
3	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 3 of 3 --
